questions.q: What is the molality of a solution containing 18g of glucose in 2000g of water?
Which of the following statements is correct about an ideal solution?

The vapour pressure of a dilute aqueous solution of glucose at a particular temperature is 750 mm of Hg. Calculate the vapour pressure of pure water at that temperature if the mole fraction of the solute is 0.1.
questions.answer: Using Raoult's law: P_solution = P_solvent * X_solvent, 750 = P_solvent * 0.9, P_solvent = 750/0.9 = 833.33 mm of Hg

questions.q: Fill in the blank: According to Raoult's law, the relative lowering of vapour pressure is equal to the _______________ of the solute.
State whether the statement is True or False: The boiling point of a solution is always higher than that of the solvent.
What is the freezing point depression constant of water if the freezing point of 0.01 m NaCl solution is -0.034 degree?
questions.answer: ΔT_f = K_f * m, 0.034 = K_f * 0.01, K_f = 3.4 K kg/mol

questions.q: The molar mass of a non-electrolyte solute is 50 g/mol. If 2g of the solute is dissolved in 100 mL of water, what will be the elevation in boiling point?
questions.answer: Molality = (2/50) * (1000/100) = 0.4 m, ΔT_b = K_b * m, assuming K_b = 0.52 K kg/mol for water, ΔT_b = 0.52 * 0.4 = 0.208 K

questions.q: Which of the following solutions will have the highest freezing point depression?
Calculate the molarity of a solution containing 5g of NaOH in 2000 mL of solution. (Molar mass of NaOH = 40 g/mol)
State whether the statement is True or False: Colligative properties depend on the nature of the solute particles.
